ops.title: Apple portal provisioning (App IDs, App Groups, Developer ID profiles) is fully scriptable via the ASC API with the OpenClaw admin key — no browser/cookiesync needed
ops.body:
Apple Developer portal provisioning for team SXKCTF23Q2 needs no browser automation: the App Store Connect API creates bundle IDs (`POST /v1/bundleIds`, platform `MAC_OS`), enables capabilities (`POST /v1/bundleIdCapabilities`, e.g. `APP_GROUPS` — yields a `SXKCTF23Q2.*` app-groups authorization), and mints Developer ID profiles (`POST /v1/profiles`, profileType `MAC_APP_DIRECT`) whose `profileContent` comes back base64 in the response.

- **Key**: `op://OpenClaw/ASC_ADMIN_KEY_CC_PRESENT/credential` (base64 .p8; key id in the item's `key id` field, issuer = same as `MACOS_NOTARY_ISSUER_ID`). The `MACOS_NOTARY_KEY` trio can READ provisioning but gets 403 `FORBIDDEN_ERROR` on writes.
- **Gotcha**: the team's Developer ID cert is type `DEVELOPER_ID_APPLICATION_G2` — a `filter[certificateType]=DEVELOPER_ID_APPLICATION` query returns empty; include both types.
- Proven 2026-07-19 creating captain-hook's two App IDs + profiles (script pattern at the time: /tmp/asc.py — JWT ES256 via pyjwt, aud `appstoreconnect-v1`); also recorded in captain-hook's cc-notes runbook. Related: [[product-name-on-user-visible-surfaces]].
